sessions.lever: pin removal -> confirmed same v0/v1 post-call mask/load swap as the func_800611A4 family wall (see memory/wip/func_800611A4 for full cc1psx calibration + 12-lever sweep).

reviewer.evidence: Pin-free body is plain pure C (no pins/__asm__/coercion). Confirmed NOT byte-matching (score 9 = v0/v1 swap), so not applied to src/.

remaining_gap.summary: Identical wall to func_800611A4: all 9 diffs are a v0<->v1 register-name swap on the post-call mask(0xC06013)/load cluster. Build structurally byte-identical to target otherwise. Load-temp wants $v0 (target) but GCC gives it $v1 and the CSE'd mask constant $v0.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Reachable only via the $2/$3 pins (forbidden) or a regfix subst (forbidden). cc1psx calibration in the func_800611A4 WIP proves the swap is unreachable from correct pure C.

family_ref: memory/wip/func_800611A4/ — same post-call mask/load cluster; cc1psx-confirmed RA wall. func_800618B4 (parked), func_80061250, func_80061454, func_800614E0, func_8006156C, func_80061658 are siblings. A family-wide lever retires all.
next_hypotheses: Solve as a FAMILY via the func_800611A4 next_hypotheses (permuter from floor-9 base; check if the mask constants are legitimately non-constant in the original; instrumented-cc1 PRIO dump).
If the $2/$3 pins are the canonical finished form for this whole render-state family, USER canonical-asm authorization would close all of them at once.
